dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for sick person.
Good afternoon, I'm Jordan, and this is Irene. We're EMTs with the rescue squad. What brought you to call 911 today?
We found him in bed-he's very lethargic and unresponsive. He hasn't been eating well for a year.
When did you first notice the change in his condition?
He was fine last night, but this morning we could not wake him up properly. He doesn't speak at all now.
He's not truly alert-he's unresponsive but does have some eye opening. Verbal responses are absent.
He opens his eyes to pain stimulus, but his verbal response is minimal, just moaning.
He attempts to move an arm when I press gently-some motor response to pain.
He gives non-coherent sounds-he continues to moan with no coherent words.
Airway is patent for now. No signs of obstruction. Let's move to breathing.
Breathing is 16 per minute with asymmetric effort, but chest movement appears to be relatively equal.
Pulse is weak but present, 98. Skin is cool, dry, and discolored-possible pallor.
16:24 update-his pulse is still 98.
His respiration is steady at 16-showing no acute distress.
Blood pressure is initially 68/40 but later improved slightly to 84/60.
Oxygen saturation is 100% on room air-breathing is currently adequate.
Blood glucose reading is 415-very high, consistent with hyperglycemia.
EKG shows atrial fibrillation at 98 bpm-he has no known history of this.
Manual blood pressure is 84 over 60-still hypotensive overall.
Skin is essentially normal in color but cool to the touch and dry with delayed cap refill.
Pupil sizes as best I can tell are not clearly distinguishable. No neck masses or rigidity noted.
Oral cavity has thick secretions but no visible drainage or hemorrhage.
Breath sounds are clear but he does appear underweight.
No visual deformities noted on extremities, but there is pronounced weight loss.
Patient is aphasic, not following commands, refusing fluids. Family reports he's been losing weight for over a year.
Allergies have not been reported by the patient or family.
He was insulin-dependent, but no medications are currently in his possession.
He had a CVA in 2012 with residual deficits, and now this could again be neurological or hyperglycemia-related.
He underwent some resistance to taking fluids-family tried to get him to drink Ensure which he would not take.
There's no verbal report of pain and no behaviors indicating distress-score remains at 0.
Attempted to start a 20G IV in left arm but was unsuccessful after two tries-still looking for alternative access.
4-lead EKG applied for continuous monitoring of the atrial fibrillation.
Per our findings, he matches the medical protocol for altered mental status. Transport is necessary for VCU.
GCS is 11-EYE 4, VERBAL 2, MOTOR 5 as documented.
Current concerns include possible sepsis given his hyperglycemia, hypotension, and AMS-we'll keep close monitoring.
Transport destination for this conscious patient is VCU Health Systems; trauma center alerted.
He's loaded onto the Reeves stretcher, and all securing straps are fastened and tightened.
He is now on the hospital stretcher. Condition remains the same.
Pulse is still stable at 98 during the manual re-check.
His breathing rate remains at 16, unlabored-no marked respiratory distress.
Blood pressure initially measured at 84/60 and remains so upon a second manual measurement.
Recheck of blood sugar maintains at 415 mg/dL-risk of hyperglycemia remains.
Oxygen saturation is unchanged at 100% while on room air.
Protocol continues for altered mental status and hyperglycemia for stabilization during transport.
12 lead EKG obtained at 16:04 for further cardiac clarification.
We are now in transport mode-recheck vitals and monitoring status every five minutes.
Local emergency department has been updated on condition and 12-lead findings.
Patient's documentation bundle has been handed over and signed off by nurse on-site.
Arrival vitals are consistent-no changes during transport thus far.
Patient is securely on board ambulance-ECG still running.
No indication of pain reported or observed-score remains 0.
Pulse remains at 98, blood pressure at 84/60, respiration 16, SpO2 at 100, glucose 415.
Protocol for altered mental status is in effect for transport.
We are now transitioning from EMS stretcher with sheet lift to hospital cot upon arrival.
All parameters remain unchanged-vitals have been stable throughout.
Atrial fibrillation was identified via EKG performed and will be detailed to ED.
Patient is currently safe and secured in ambulance-transport is proceeding as planned.
